f17e143f-3246-5ffa-b43a-095557fd12d6.html: A language variant of Racket to help you debug program

f16d1031-ea3a-5fe8-b3f0-775ea3c36f21.title: dependent type languages
f16d1031-ea3a-5fe8-b3f0-775ea3c36f21.html:
The followings are dependent type languages I try to create.

  • semi macro as type for dependent type theory work, by using local-expand and syntax-property
  • typical try macro as type and learn how to do termination check
  • incr inductive based on introduction/elimination rules
  • inductive try to build inductive language

3bc6193b-4d4b-51b4-a0c5-59cd230a1f89.html: Tiny Kubernetes to show how its network work

7b73f4f2-d7a8-523d-8e85-9caeaa4aa087.html:
Library for interacting with LLVM IR in pure Go. As a contributor, I majorly contribute to

  • 128-bits floating number
  • support of llvm 10.0/11.0
  • support of llvm 12.0(working)
  • document

4b74168f-512f-558f-9315-7333c7edfcdb.title: macro is not magic
4b74168f-512f-558f-9315-7333c7edfcdb.html: Present how macro work. The idea of macro is simple: a function operating AST. By operating AST, we can create new syntax for certain usage.
